headlineSummary: HN leans negative on OpenAI, while Anthropic stays mixed with both praise and criticism; Microsoft Copilot and Google Gemini have limited evidence and read mostly neutral [E6][E3][E14][E12].

ranking.summary: OpenAI skews negative overall: comments criticize missed launches, weak voice mode, and product economics, though there is some praise for Codex and ChatGPT working well [E6][E7][E8][E9][E10][E13][E15].
Microsoft Copilot has limited evidence and reads slightly negative, with one comment implying the Copilot-made proof of concept was a rough starting point [E14].
Anthropic is mixed but fairly active in discussion, with praise for Claude’s capability and coding use alongside complaints about hype, quality, and pricing [E1][E2][E3][E4][E5][E11][E13].
Google Gemini has limited evidence and a mildly positive note from one comparison saying Gemini 2.5 Flash performed better than the alternative [E12].

evidence.excerpt: I guess they really do eat their own dogfood and vibe code their way through it without care for technical debt? In a way, it’s a good challenge, but it’s fairly painful to watch the current state of the project

annotations.rationale: In the Claude Code story context, this is a clear criticism of Claude Code's engineering quality and technical debt.

evidence.excerpt: I don't get the hype around Claude Code. There's nothing new or unique. The real strength are the models.

annotations.rationale: The excerpt criticizes Claude Code as overhyped and unoriginal, but also credits Anthropic's models as the real strength, yielding a mixed overall stance.

evidence.excerpt: Claude wrote a full FreeBSD remote kernel RCE with root shell

annotations.rationale: The title presents Claude as capable of producing a sophisticated exploit, which reflects positively on model capability.

evidence.excerpt: A reminder: this bug was also found by Claude (specifically, by Nicholas Carlini at Anthropic).

annotations.rationale: The comment credits Claude/Anthropic with finding the bug, a favorable assessment of capability.

evidence.excerpt: Everything on HN is people doing unpaid promotional work for Anthropic, just talking about all the promise Claude holds and all the various ways you could be spending more money on Claude. bored aimless vibes.

annotations.rationale: The commenter strongly disparages Anthropic coverage as promotional hype and frames Claude as a money sink, indicating strong negativity.

evidence.excerpt: The OpenAI graveyard: All the deals and products that haven't happened

annotations.rationale: Calling it an 'OpenAI graveyard' frames OpenAI's unreleased or failed efforts very negatively.

evidence.excerpt: It's missing the voice mode that never reached the level they demoed, and then gradually went to shit from that.

annotations.rationale: In the OpenAI story context, this is a harsh criticism of OpenAI's voice mode quality versus its demo.

evidence.excerpt: OpenAI isn't shutting down Codex or ChatGPT, because those were launches that they did that actually worked!

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says Codex and ChatGPT 'actually worked,' which is praise for successful OpenAI launches.

evidence.excerpt: A lot of this stuff seems to just quietly never happen once the ink on the PR puff dries.

annotations.rationale: In the OpenAI graveyard discussion, this accuses OpenAI of making announcements that do not materialize.

evidence.excerpt: With them most likely losing money on inference (+model training + salaries + building data centers), I can't see why they would want more compute and more products

annotations.rationale: Within the OpenAI thread, this critiques OpenAI's economics and product strategy as unsound.

evidence.excerpt: Claude Sonnet is in 5th place with 1.05T tokens. Which isn't super suprising as StepFun is ~about 5% the price of Sonnet.

annotations.rationale: The comment frames Claude Sonnet as much more expensive than a rival, a mild negative on Anthropic pricing.

evidence.excerpt: Yet when I tried it it did absymal compared to Gemini 2.5 Flash

annotations.rationale: The commenter says the competing model performed badly relative to Gemini 2.5 Flash, implying Gemini performed better.

evidence.excerpt: I'm a data-scientist now, and a fan of claude code for implementing things. But I have to say, I'm constantly surprised by how "dumb" chatgpt is as a math research partner.

annotations.rationale: Calling oneself 'a fan of claude code' is direct mild praise for Anthropic's coding product.
Describing ChatGPT as 'dumb' for math research is strong criticism of OpenAI's model quality in that use case.

evidence.excerpt: taking the POC someone slapped together in Copilot (it works!) and putting that solution in production.

annotations.rationale: The phrase suggests a hastily assembled Copilot-generated proof of concept, implying low-quality output.

evidence.excerpt: it builds on a strong underlying library (the OpenAI Codex implementation)

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly calls the OpenAI Codex implementation a 'strong underlying library,' which is direct praise.
